reasoning: The baseline 3-month Treasury yield for the June 22 comparison is likely the June 20 value of 4.39%, down from 4.45% earlier in June. In the very short term (June 29), daily volatility and mean reversion give a near coin-flip chance of the yield ticking back above 4.39%. By July 22, the upcoming July 30 FOMC meeting may lock in lower rates if a cut occurs, bringing the probability down slightly. Further out, futures markets are confidently pricing in 1 to 3 rate cuts by year-end 2025 (extensively cited at ~88% chance of multiple cuts). This structural downward drift limits the probability of yields holding above 4.39% for the September and December dates. The long-term probabilities (28% and 20%) reflect the tail-risk that inflation flares up and forces the Fed to unexpectedly hold or raise rates, in accordance with the guidance to maintain uncertainty for distant forecasts.
